ask: We need a way for users to dispute a ghost score they think is wrong.

story.title: Ghost Score Dispute – Submit a Dispute on a Ghost Classification
story.story: As a job seeker, I want to flag a ghost score I believe is incorrect, so that I can signal my disagreement and help improve the model's accuracy.

acs.name: Dispute CTA Visible on Analyze Page
acs.given: a job seeker is viewing the Analyze page for a posting classified as GHOST
acs.when: the ghost score and rationale are rendered
thens: A 'Dispute this score' link or button will be visible beneath the ghost score verdict.
The CTA will be styled in a calm, non-alarmist manner consistent with GUARDRAILS.md (no red, no urgent language).
The CTA will not appear for postings classified as REAL.

acs.name: Dispute Form Displayed
acs.given: a job seeker is on the Analyze page for a GHOST-classified posting
acs.when: the job seeker clicks 'Dispute this score'
thens: A dispute form will appear inline or in a modal without navigating away from the page.
The form will include a required reason-selection field with at least the options: 'I applied and received a response', 'I know this role is active', and 'Other'.
The form will include an optional free-text field (max 500 characters) for additional context.
The form will display the one-sentence model rationale so the user can reference it while writing their dispute.

acs.name: Dispute Successfully Submitted
acs.given: a job seeker has completed the dispute form with a selected reason
acs.when: the job seeker submits the form
thens: A dispute record will be written to a new `ghost_score_disputes` table containing: `posting_id`, `user_id`, `reason_code`, `free_text` (nullable), `submitted_at`, and `status` defaulting to 'pending'.
A success confirmation message will be displayed to the user acknowledging receipt of the dispute.
The 'Dispute this score' CTA will be replaced with a 'Dispute submitted' indicator for that posting for the remainder of the session.
The ghost score verdict displayed on the page will remain unchanged and will not be altered by submission alone.

acs.name: Duplicate Dispute Prevention
acs.given: a job seeker has already submitted a dispute for a specific posting
acs.when: the job seeker revisits the Analyze page for that same posting
thens: The 'Dispute this score' CTA will be replaced with a 'Dispute submitted' indicator.
The dispute form will not be accessible for that posting.
No duplicate record will be written to `ghost_score_disputes`.

acs.name: Form Submission Failure
acs.given: a job seeker has completed the dispute form
acs.when: the form submission fails due to a network or server error
thens: An inline error message will be displayed informing the user that the submission did not go through.
The form will remain populated with the user's input so they can retry without re-entering data.
No partial record will be persisted to `ghost_score_disputes`.

assumptions_carried: A1 — Dispute review workflow (human or automated) is out of scope for this story; `status` field is included to support a future review flow but no review UI is built here.
A2 — No email confirmation is sent to the user upon dispute submission; notification infrastructure does not yet exist per C4, and adding it is a separate story.
A3 — The dispute feature applies only to GHOST-classified postings; disputing a REAL classification is not in scope and is assumed to be a separate backlog item.
A4 — Authentication is assumed to be in place; `user_id` is available from the active session when writing the dispute record.
A5 — The ghost score displayed to the user will not be dynamically updated as a result of a dispute; any re-scoring logic is a downstream concern outside this story's scope.
A6 — The `ghost_score_disputes` table does not yet exist and will need to be created as part of this story's implementation.
A7 — Dispute data will be used to retrain or audit the classifier in a future initiative; that pipeline is explicitly out of scope here.

errors: ACE numbering is not sequential from 1: [4, 5]
warnings: AC1: WHEN contains 'and' — likely two ACs.

clarity.reasoning: The generated story uses clear, plain product language with a named persona (job seeker), a single-action 'I want' statement, and unambiguous AC names. It is at least as clear as the reference, with slightly more detail on UI behavior.

testability.reasoning: All ACs follow GIVEN/WHEN/THEN structure with observable, future-tense outcomes. The generated story adds two additional edge cases (form submission failure, duplicate on revisit vs. same session) that are more thorough than the reference, making it highly testable.

scope_fidelity.reasoning: The generated story stays within the core ask and does not invent unrelated features. However, it adds a free-text field (AC2) which the reference explicitly flags as NOT in scope (A2 says preset list, not free text), and it specifies a concrete database schema (`ghost_score_disputes` table with specific columns), which goes slightly beyond what was asked. These are minor but notable deviations from the reference's intentional constraints.

completeness.reasoning: The generated story covers all reference ACs plus adds error handling (ACE5), revisit-based duplicate prevention (ACE4 vs. same-session only in reference), and seven well-articulated assumptions covering authentication, no email notification, no re-scoring, table creation, and future pipeline. This exceeds the reference in completeness.

grounding.reasoning: All claims are tied to context (GUARDRAILS.md reference, C4 for notification infrastructure, session-based user_id). Assumptions are clearly prefixed A1–A7 and flag ungrounded items (e.g., table doesn't exist yet, review workflow is future). No unsubstantiated claims are made without flagging them as assumptions.
